title: 5 Steps to Get How Kenyan Creators Get Paid Internationally Working for Your Store
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: You're a digital creator from Ghana, but your bank account restrictions prevent you from using PayPal or Stripe. This makes it nearly impossible to sell your ebooks, courses, or art to international customers. You've tried reaching out to popular payment processors, but their 'no account creation for you' policy has turned you away. You're not alone in this struggle. Many creators in your situation face a daunting reality: their passion and creativity are being stifled by outdated payment systems. The good news is that there's a solution, and it's more accessible than you think.
The Full List With Context

To get paid internationally as a Kenyan creator (and Ghanaian creator like yourself), you'll need to adopt a multi-step approach. Here's the full list with context: (1) Set up a business entity to separate personal and business income. This helps you maintain a professional image and protects your personal assets. (2) Choose a reliable payment processor that supports your geographical location and accepts a wide range of cryptocurrencies. NOWPayments is a popular option. (3) Create a website or online store using a suitable e-commerce platform, such as Wix or Shopify. (4) Develop a marketing strategy to attract international customers and promote your digital products. This can include social media marketing, email marketing, or influencer outreach. (5) Use a secure delivery and payment system, such as Unchained Commerce's Full Stack Bundle, to ensure smooth transactions and automated delivery. Lastly, (6) continuously evaluate and improve your payment system to adapt to changes in the market and customer needs.
Why Each Item on This List Matters

Each item on this list is crucial to getting paid internationally as a Kenyan creator. Setting up a business entity (item 1) helps you avoid personal asset risks and maintain a professional image. Choosing a reliable payment processor (item 2) ensures you can accept a wide range of cryptocurrencies and cater to your geographical location. Creating a website or online store (item 3) provides an essential platform for promoting and selling your digital products. Developing a marketing strategy (item 4) is vital to attracting international customers and increasing sales. Using a secure delivery and payment system (item 5) ensures smooth transactions and automated delivery. Lastly, continuously evaluating and improving your payment system (item 6) helps you adapt to changes in the market and customer needs, ensuring the long-term success of your business.
The Ones Most Creators Get Wrong

When it comes to getting paid internationally, many creators make common mistakes that can hinder their success. One of the most significant errors is neglecting to set up a business entity, which can lead to personal asset risks and an unprofessional image. Another mistake is choosing an unreliable payment processor, which can result in failed transactions and lost sales. Failing to develop a marketing strategy can also lead to a lack of visibility and a limited customer base. Lastly, neglecting to use a secure delivery and payment system can result in complications during transactions and affect customer trust. By avoiding these common mistakes, you can set yourself up for success and achieve your goals as a digital creator.
The blockchain has no map of restricted countries — a wallet in Lagos processes identically to one in London.
How to Prioritise What to Implement First

Prioritizing what to implement first can be challenging, especially when faced with multiple tasks and requirements. However, as a digital creator, it's essential to focus on the most critical aspects that will drive your success. First, ensure you have a reliable payment processor (item 2) that supports your geographical location and accepts a wide range of cryptocurrencies. Next, create a website or online store (item 3) to promote and sell your digital products. Developing a marketing strategy (item 4) should follow shortly, as it will help you attract international customers and increase sales. Finally, use a secure delivery and payment system (item 5) to ensure smooth transactions and automated delivery. By focusing on these critical aspects first, you'll set yourself up for long-term success and increase your chances of getting paid internationally.
Putting the List Into Action

Now that you have the full list with context, you're ready to put it into action. Start by setting up a business entity to separate personal and business income. Choose a reliable payment processor that supports your geographical location and accepts a wide range of cryptocurrencies. Create a website or online store using a suitable e-commerce platform, such as Wix or Shopify. Develop a marketing strategy to attract international customers and promote your digital products. Use a secure delivery and payment system, such as Unchained Commerce's Full Stack Bundle, to ensure smooth transactions and automated delivery. Lastly, continuously evaluate and improve your payment system to adapt to changes in the market and customer needs. By following these steps and prioritizing your tasks effectively, you'll be well on your way to getting paid internationally as a Kenyan creator.

Your Action Checklist

Create your NOWPayments account and add wallet addresses for your primary coins. Deploy the Core Shop Engine with your product files and NOWPayments credentials. Publish your store and share the URL with your existing audience. Track your first transactions in the Admin Dashboard and confirm the conversion rate you are getting from P2P exchange.
